Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a provided event log of a Procure to Pay P2P process identify anomalous behavior compared to standard P2P best practices and explain why these anomalies are problematic

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Recall Define Standard P2P Process Flow Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order based on approved PR Receive Goods based on PO 3 way match component 1 Vendor Invoice Received 3 way match component 2 Match Invoice to PO 3 way match PO Goods Receipt Invoice Release Payment only after successful match and goods received Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Each Case in the Event Log

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Check PR Approve PO Goods Invoice Match Pay Close Conclusion Perfectly standard No anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Issue Purchase Order Anomaly 1 PO issued BEFORE PR is approved Release Payment Anomaly 2 Payment released BEFORE Goods received BEFORE Invoice matched to PO Approve Purchase Requisition Anomaly 3 PR approved AFTER PO issued and Payment released Receive Goods Anomaly 4 Goods received AFTER payment Conclusion Highly anomalous Out of sequence in multiple critical ways pre mature PO premature payment retroactive approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Release Payment Anomaly 1 Payment released BEFORE Invoice received BEFORE Goods received BEFORE Invoice matched Vendor Invoice Received Receive Goods Anomaly 2 Goods received AFTER payment Match Invoice to PO Anomaly 3 Matching happens AFTER payment is already released Conclusion Highly anomalous Paying before receiving goods or an invoice is a major compliance and financial risk

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Receive Goods Anomaly 1 Goods received BEFORE PO is issued Issue Purchase Order Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case Conclusion Anomalous Receiving goods before a formal PO exists is a maverick buying compliance risk though the rest of the flow invoice match pay is okay

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings and Structure the Response Introduction Briefly state the standard P2P process to establish the baseline for comparison Case by Case Analysis Go through each case highlighting the normal flow vs the anomalies Identify Anomalies by Category Thematic Approach Instead of just listing cases group the anomalies by the type of violation and explain why it is problematic This adds more value Category 1 Skipping Approvals Out of Order Authorization Case 1002 PO before PR approval Category 2 Premature Payment Paying before 3 Way Match Cases 1002 1003 Releasing payment before goods invoice match Category 3 Receiving Goods without a Purchase Order Case 1004 Goods received before PO issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explain the Why it is problematic Business Impact Financial Risk Paying for undelivered goods or fake invoices Compliance Audit Risk Violation of segregation of duties lack of proper authorization Operational Inefficiency Rework disputes with vendors inventory tracking issues

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure I explicitly state that Case 1001 is the standard It is good to have a baseline Refining Case 1002 It is the worst offender Payment was released on 02 03 at 07 15 before the PR was even approved at 08 00 on the same day This is a severe Segregation of Duties SoD and internal control failure Refining Case 1003 Payment was made almost a week before goods were received High risk of fraud or losing money if the vendor goes bankrupt or never delivers Refining Case 1004 Receiving goods before a PO is common in some emergency scenarios maverick buying but it bypasses controls However at least they did not pay before getting the goods invoice It is a lower severity than 1002 1003 but still an anomaly against best practices

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Prompt Requirements Analyzed the event log Yes Identified sequences that seem unusual anomalous Yes Relied on general knowledge of P2P Yes referenced 3 way match authorization etc Explained why they might be problematic Yes
